name: HW3 From-Scratch
description: Grading spec for HW3 from-scratch implementations of convolution, pooling, and training

tests.name: conv2d_forward_shape
tests.description: Output shape is correct for various input/kernel/stride/padding/dilation combinations
tests.code:
# Test 1: Basic case
x = torch.randn(2, 3, 32, 32)
w = torch.randn(16, 3, 3, 3)
result = conv2d_forward(x, w, padding=1, stride=1, dilation=1)
expected_shape = (2, 16, 32, 32)
assert result.shape == expected_shape, f'Expected {expected_shape}, got {result.shape}'

# Test 2: Stride=2
result = conv2d_forward(x, w, padding=1, stride=2, dilation=1)
expected_shape = (2, 16, 16, 16)
assert result.shape == expected_shape, f'Expected {expected_shape}, got {result.shape}'

# Test 3: No padding
result = conv2d_forward(x, w, padding=0, stride=1, dilation=1)
expected_shape = (2, 16, 30, 30)
assert result.shape == expected_shape, f'Expected {expected_shape}, got {result.shape}'

# Test 4: Dilation=2
w2 = torch.randn(16, 3, 3, 3)
result = conv2d_forward(x, w2, padding=2, stride=1, dilation=2)
expected_shape = (2, 16, 32, 32)
assert result.shape == expected_shape, f'Expected {expected_shape}, got {result.shape}'

print('conv2d_forward_shape: PASSED')

tests.name: conv2d_forward_values
tests.description: Output values match F.conv2d for various configurations
tests.code:
# Test forward pass values
torch.manual_seed(42)
x = torch.randn(1, 3, 8, 8)
w = torch.randn(8, 3, 3, 3)
b = torch.randn(8)

# Test with padding
result_student = conv2d_forward(x, w, b=b, padding=1, stride=1, dilation=1)
result_pytorch = F.conv2d(x, w, bias=b, padding=1, stride=1, dilation=1)
assert torch.allclose(result_student, result_pytorch, atol=1e-5), 'Results do not match with padding=1'

# Test with stride=2
result_student = conv2d_forward(x, w, b=b, padding=1, stride=2, dilation=1)
result_pytorch = F.conv2d(x, w, bias=b, padding=1, stride=2, dilation=1)
assert torch.allclose(result_student, result_pytorch, atol=1e-5), 'Results do not match with stride=2'

# Test without bias
result_student = conv2d_forward(x, w, b=None, padding=0, stride=1, dilation=1)
result_pytorch = F.conv2d(x, w, bias=None, padding=0, stride=1, dilation=1)
assert torch.allclose(result_student, result_pytorch, atol=1e-5), 'Results do not match without bias'

# Test with dilation
result_student = conv2d_forward(x, w, b=None, padding=1, stride=1, dilation=2)
result_pytorch = F.conv2d(x, w, bias=None, padding=1, stride=1, dilation=2)
assert torch.allclose(result_student, result_pytorch, atol=1e-5), 'Results do not match with dilation=2'

print('conv2d_forward_values: PASSED')

tests.name: conv2d_backward_x
tests.code:
torch.manual_seed(42)
x = torch.randn(1, 3, 8, 8, requires_grad=True)
w = torch.randn(8, 3, 3, 3, requires_grad=False)
b = None

dy = torch.randn(1, 8, 8, 8)
dx, dw, db = conv2d_backward(dy, x, w, b, padding=1, stride=1, dilation=1)

x_pt = x.clone().detach().requires_grad_(True)
y_pt = F.conv2d(x_pt, w, bias=b, padding=1, stride=1, dilation=1)
y_pt.backward(dy)

assert torch.allclose(dx, x_pt.grad, atol=1e-4), f'x.grad mismatch: {(dx - x_pt.grad).abs().max().item()}'
print('conv2d_backward_x: PASSED')

tests.name: conv2d_backward_w
tests.code:
torch.manual_seed(42)
x = torch.randn(1, 3, 8, 8, requires_grad=False)
w = torch.randn(8, 3, 3, 3, requires_grad=True)
b = None

dy = torch.randn(1, 8, 8, 8)
dx, dw, db = conv2d_backward(dy, x, w, b, padding=1, stride=1, dilation=1)

w_pt = w.clone().detach().requires_grad_(True)
y_pt = F.conv2d(x, w_pt, bias=b, padding=1, stride=1, dilation=1)
y_pt.backward(dy)

assert torch.allclose(dw, w_pt.grad, atol=1e-4), f'w.grad mismatch: {(dw - w_pt.grad).abs().max().item()}'
print('conv2d_backward_w: PASSED')

tests.name: conv2d_backward_b
tests.code:
torch.manual_seed(42)
x = torch.randn(1, 3, 8, 8, requires_grad=False)
w = torch.randn(8, 3, 3, 3, requires_grad=False)
b = torch.randn(8, requires_grad=True)

dy = torch.randn(1, 8, 8, 8)
dx, dw, db = conv2d_backward(dy, x, w, b, padding=1, stride=1, dilation=1)

b_pt = b.clone().detach().requires_grad_(True)
y_pt = F.conv2d(x, w, bias=b_pt, padding=1, stride=1, dilation=1)
y_pt.backward(dy)

assert db is not None, 'db should not be None when b is provided'
assert torch.allclose(db, b_pt.grad, atol=1e-4), f'b.grad mismatch: {(db - b_pt.grad).abs().max().item()}'
print('conv2d_backward_b: PASSED')

tests.name: max_pool2d_forward
tests.description: Output matches F.max_pool2d for various configurations
tests.code:
torch.manual_seed(42)
x = torch.randn(2, 3, 8, 8)

# Test 1: Basic 2x2 pooling
result_student, _ = max_pool2d_forward(x, kernel_size=2, padding=0, stride=2)
result_pytorch = F.max_pool2d(x, kernel_size=2, padding=0, stride=2)
assert torch.allclose(result_student, result_pytorch, atol=1e-5), 'Basic pooling failed'
assert result_student.shape == (2, 3, 4, 4), f'Shape mismatch: {result_student.shape}'

# Test 2: Stride defaults to kernel_size
result_student2, _ = max_pool2d_forward(x, kernel_size=3, padding=0, stride=None)
result_pytorch2 = F.max_pool2d(x, kernel_size=3, padding=0, stride=3)
assert torch.allclose(result_student2, result_pytorch2, atol=1e-5), 'Stride defaulting failed'

print('max_pool2d_forward: PASSED')

tests.name: max_pool2d_backward
tests.code:
torch.manual_seed(42)
x_pt = torch.randn(2, 3, 8, 8, requires_grad=True)

# PyTorch reference
y_pt = F.max_pool2d(x_pt, kernel_size=2, padding=0, stride=2)
dy = torch.randn_like(y_pt)
y_pt.backward(dy)
dx_ref = x_pt.grad.clone()

# Student backward
x_s = x_pt.data.clone()
dx_s = max_pool2d_backward(dy, x_s, kernel_size=2, padding=0, stride=2)

assert torch.allclose(dx_s, dx_ref, atol=1e-4), f'max_pool2d backward mismatch max diff: {(dx_s - dx_ref).abs().max().item()}'
print('max_pool2d_backward: PASSED')

tests.name: conv2d_layer
tests.description: Conv2d layer produces correct output shape and values
tests.code:
torch.manual_seed(42)
layer = Conv2d(3, 16, kernel_size=3, padding=1, stride=1)
x = torch.randn(2, 3, 8, 8)
y = layer.forward(x)
assert y.shape == (2, 16, 8, 8), f'Conv2d layer shape: {y.shape}'
print('conv2d_layer: PASSED')

tests.name: maxpool2d_layer
tests.code:
layer = MaxPool2d(kernel_size=2, stride=2)
x = torch.randn(2, 3, 8, 8)
y = layer.forward(x)
assert y.shape == (2, 3, 4, 4), f'MaxPool2d layer shape: {y.shape}'
print('maxpool2d_layer: PASSED')

tests.name: momentum_sgd
tests.description: Parameter update with momentum is correct
tests.code:
p = torch.tensor([1.0, 2.0, 3.0])
p.grad = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
opt = MomentumSGD([p], lr=0.1, momentum=0.9)
opt.step()
# After first step: v = 0.9*0 + grad = [0.1, 0.2, 0.3], p -= 0.1 * v
expected = torch.tensor([1.0 - 0.01, 2.0 - 0.02, 3.0 - 0.03])
assert torch.allclose(p, expected, atol=1e-5), f'First step: {p} vs {expected}'

# Second step
p.grad = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3])
opt.step()
# v = 0.9*[0.1,0.2,0.3] + [0.1,0.2,0.3] = [0.19, 0.38, 0.57]
# p -= 0.1 * v
expected2 = expected - 0.1 * torch.tensor([0.19, 0.38, 0.57])
assert torch.allclose(p, expected2, atol=1e-5), f'Second step: {p} vs {expected2}'
print('momentum_sgd: PASSED')

tests.name: convnet_forward
tests.description: ConvNet forward produces correct output shape
tests.code:
model = ConvNet()
x = torch.randn(2, 3, 32, 32)
y = model.forward(x)
assert y.shape[0] == 2, f'Batch dim: {y.shape[0]}'
assert y.shape[1] == 10, f'Output classes: {y.shape[1]}'
print('convnet_forward: PASSED')

tests.name: cifar_accuracy
tests.code:
# Test that the model can learn — train on tiny subset and check it beats random
import torchvision, torchvision.transforms as transforms
tf = transforms.Compose([transforms.ToTensor(), transforms.Normalize((0.5,0.5,0.5),(0.5,0.5,0.5))])
train_ds = torchvision.datasets.CIFAR10('./data', train=True, download=False, transform=tf)
test_ds = torchvision.datasets.CIFAR10('./data', train=False, download=False, transform=tf)
train_sub = torch.utils.data.Subset(train_ds, range(2000))
test_sub = torch.utils.data.Subset(test_ds, range(500))
train_ldr = torch.utils.data.DataLoader(train_sub, batch_size=32, shuffle=True)
test_ldr = torch.utils.data.DataLoader(test_sub, batch_size=256)

_model = ConvNet()
_opt = MomentumSGD(_model.parameters(), lr=0.01, momentum=0.9)
for _ep in range(3):
    _model.train()
    for _x, _y in train_ldr:
        logits = _model.forward(_x)
        loss = cross_entropy_loss(logits, _y)
        _model.zero_grad()
        loss.backward()
        _opt.step()

_model.eval()
_correct = 0; _total = 0
with torch.no_grad():
    for _x, _y in test_ldr:
        _preds = _model.forward(_x).argmax(dim=1)
        _correct += (_preds == _y).sum().item()
        _total += _y.size(0)
_acc = _correct / _total
print(f'CIFAR-10 mini-test accuracy: {_acc:.2%}')
assert _acc >= 0.25, f'Model should beat random (10%), got {_acc:.2%}'

tests.name: cifar_accuracy_bonus
tests.code:
# Bonus: check if the mini-trained model from previous test achieved high accuracy
assert _acc >= 0.35, f'Bonus: need >=35% on mini-test, got {_acc:.2%}'
print(f'BONUS: CIFAR-10 mini-test accuracy {_acc:.2%} exceeds 45%!')
